title: Curriculum Vitae
text:
Assistant Professional Researcher (Research Faculty) University of California, Irvine
Email: rpassiat@uci.edu


My research goal is to advance a personalized understanding of brain development and mental health by identifying the neural, genetic, and environmental factors that shape individual trajectories of risk and resilience.
I develop mechanistic, person-centered models to understand how reward learning, emotion regulation, and neurodevelopmental processes differ across individuals. Using multimodal neuroimaging, computational psychiatry, and imaging genetics, my work aims to clarify the biological pathways linking brain function to psychopathology.
Ultimately, I seek to translate these insights into improved strategies for early identification, prevention, and intervention in depression, anxiety, and related mood disorders.

Noel Drury Depression Institute for Translational Depression Discoveries, University of California, Irvine
Supervisor: Diego A. Pizzagalli, PhD

title: Home
text:
Assistant Professional Researcher (Reseach Faculty) Noel Drury Depression Institute for Translational Depression Discoveries
Department of Psychiatry & Human Behavior
University of California, Irvine


My research aims to advance a personalized understanding of brain development and mental health by identifying the neural, genetic, and environmental mechanisms that shape individual trajectories of risk and resilience.
Using multimodal neuroimaging, computational modeling, and imaging genetics, I investigate how reward learning, emotion regulation, and cognitive control vary across individuals and developmental stages. My long-term goal is to develop mechanistic, person-centered models that improve early identification and intervention for depression, anxiety, and related mood disorders.




Computational psychiatry
Reward learning and anhedonia
Multimodal neuroimaging
Imaging genetics
Developmental cognitive neuroscience
Precision psychiatry





Email: rpassiat@uci.edu
Scopus ID: 57205701297
GitHub: robertapassiatore (https://github.com/robertapassiatore)
